message:
fix(list): prefer canonical tool-use count over source-file fallback

The previous logic used `.max()` to pick the higher of canonical vs
fallback tool-use counts, which could over-report when the fallback
heuristic (source-file grep) found false positives in session files
that had already been parsed correctly by the canonical extractor.

Switch to a conditional: use the canonical count when it is positive
(i.e., the structured parser found real tool invocations), and only
fall back to the source-file grep when canonical extraction yielded
zero. This avoids double-counting and keeps the fallback as a safety
net for providers whose format the canonical parser does not yet cover.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
